# awsprof - AWS profile switcher CLI

`awsprof` is a command-line tool (binary `awsprof` / `awsprof.exe`) for picking
an AWS profile to log in as, from the list of profiles available on the machine.
It reads the standard AWS shared config files and lets you select/activate one.

> Status: early / in design. Scaffolding, docs, and release pipeline exist; the
> concrete command surface is being defined. The commands below describe the
> intended shape and will change as the design settles.

## Installation

```bash
# macOS / Linux via Homebrew (once the first release is cut)
brew tap payfacto/tap
brew install payfacto/tap/awsprof

# Pre-built binaries (Linux/macOS/Windows, amd64+arm64)
# Download an archive from https://github.com/payfacto/awsprof-cli/releases
# and place the `awsprof` binary on your PATH.

# Go developers
go install github.com/payfacto/awsprof-cli@latest

# Or build locally
go build -o awsprof .
```

## Profile discovery

Profiles come from the standard AWS locations:

- `~/.aws/config` - `[profile NAME]` and `[sso-session NAME]` sections
- `~/.aws/credentials` - `[NAME]` sections
- Overrides: `AWS_CONFIG_FILE`, `AWS_SHARED_CREDENTIALS_FILE`

`awsprof` reads these files. It does not write long-lived credentials into a
config of its own.

## Commands (intended shape, in design)

```
awsprof                 # interactive: pick a profile from the list
awsprof list            # print the available profiles
awsprof use PROFILE     # select/activate a specific profile
awsprof --version       # print version
awsprof --help          # help
```

The details still to be decided: how a profile is "activated" (export
`AWS_PROFILE`, run `aws sso login --profile`, print credentials), the output
formats, and whether the bare command opens an interactive picker or prints
help. Prefer explicit subcommands in scripts once they exist; do not rely on
the bare interactive form in automation.

## Notes for AI agents

- This project was seeded from an unrelated Bitbucket CLI (`bb`) as a template.
  It has no Bitbucket functionality; ignore any residual references.
- There is no Go source yet - the command surface is being designed. Check
  `.context/HANDOFF.md` and `.context/specs/` for the current plan before
  assuming a command exists.
- Once implemented, the CLI is expected to exit non-zero on error and write
  errors to stderr, keeping stdout clean for machine-readable output.
